stack.storage: object_store (S3/Local) — backup-only for bytes; SPARQ authoritative for RDF

roadmap.milestone: M1 — coherent compiling vertical slice (axum boot + DPoP auth middleware via solid-oidc-verifier + GET/HEAD/PUT over a Store trait, SPARQ-authoritative / object_store-backup seams, Turtle/JSON-LD parsing, unit tests)
roadmap.note: EXPERIMENTAL parallel track — does NOT replace and must NEVER touch the production TypeScript prod-solid-server (the live, supported server). Architecture per the maintainer's directive + the Rust-migration spike: SPARQ is the AUTHORITATIVE source for RDF data AND access-control evaluation (queried over its HTTP API); object_store/S3 is BACKUP-ONLY for resource bytes. DPoP/Solid-OIDC verification is delegated to the standalone solid-oidc-verifier crate (consumed as a git dependency) — auth is NOT reimplemented here. M1 ships the vertical slice with clean trait seams (Store / SparqClient / BlobStore, each with an in-memory test impl) + unit tests; the verifier is consumed at its M1 (StaticJwksProvider + InMemoryReplayStore test seams). DEFERRED to later slices: full WAC evaluation, the full LDP verb set (POST/DELETE/PATCH/Range/conditional/conneg negotiation beyond Turtle/JSON-LD), notifications (WebSocketChannel2023), reconciliation, the live SPARQ HTTP client (needs a running SPARQ), and live JWKS (needs the verifier's M2 network adapters). Code-level `M2:`-marked seam comments flag where the next slice plugs in.
